title: Kasi Viswanathar temple, Tenkasi
tagline: Kasi Viswanathar temple, Tenkasi — spiritual town of Tamil Nadu

heroImage.alt: Kasi Viswanathar temple, Tenkasi, Tamil Nadu

overview.short: Kasi Viswanathar Temple in Tenkasi, a city in Tenkasi district in the South Indian state of Tamil Nadu, is dedicated to the Hindu god Shiva.
overview.description: Kasi Viswanathar Temple in Tenkasi, a city in Tenkasi district in the South Indian state of Tamil Nadu, is dedicated to the Hindu god Shiva. Constructed in the Tamil style of architecture, the temple is believed to have been built by Pandyan ruler Parakrama Pandyan during the 13th century, with later additions from Madurai Nayaks. Shiva is worshipped as Kasi Viswanathar and his consort Parvathi as Ulagamman. A granite wall surrounds the temple, enclosing all its shrines.

topPlaces.name: Tenkasi
topPlaces.description: Tenkasi (Tamil: [t̪eŋɡaːsi]) is a City and the headquarters of the Tenkasi district in Indian state of Tamil Nadu.Tenkasi is the largest town in Tenkasi district Being one of the most significant spiritual and cultural places in South Tamilnadu, Tenkasi houses The Kutraleeshwarar temple (Chitra Sabha), one of the five sabhas of Lord Nataraja, an incarnation of Lord Shiva. Tamil poet Thirikooda Rasappa Kavirayar describes this temple in his poem as “Kutrala Kuravanji” which means 'beauty of Kutralam'.

topPlaces.name: Kuthukalvalasai
topPlaces.description: Kuthukalvalasai is a neighborhood located in Tenkasi, Tamil Nadu, India, 1 km away from District headquarters Tenkasi. It is located at the foot of the Western Ghats and Courtallam hills.

topPlaces.name: Melagaram
topPlaces.description: Melagaram is a panchayat town in Tenkasi district, in the Indian state of Tamil Nadu. The Famous Composer Thirigudarasapa Kavirayar was born in Melagaram.

topPlaces.name: Ilanji
topPlaces.description: Ilanji is a panchayat town in Tenkasi district, in the Indian state of Tamil Nadu. It is located between the towns Tenkasi and Shencottah.

topPlaces.name: Piranoor
topPlaces.description: Piranoor is a Panchayat Village of Tenkasi Block and Shenkottai Taluk. in Tenkasi district, Tamil Nadu, India, in the foothills of the Western Ghats.

topPlaces.name: Aluthakanniar River
topPlaces.description: Aluthakanniar is a river flowing in the Tirunelveli district of the Indian state of Tamil Nadu. It is one of the tributaries of Chittar River, which joins Tamiraparani near Tirunelveli.

topPlaces.name: Kottakulam
topPlaces.description: Kottakulam is a panchayat town in Tirunelveli district in the Indian state of Tamil Nadu. Kottakulam is aka Sumaitheernthapuram.

topPlaces.name: Courtallam
topPlaces.description: Courtallam, natively spelt Kutrālam or Kuttālam, is a panchayat town situated at a mean elevation of 160 m (520 ft) in the foothills of the Western Ghats in Tenkasi district of Tamil Nadu, India. The Coutrallam Falls on the Chittar River is a major tourist attraction.

items.note: Tenkasi (Tamil: [t̪eŋɡaːsi]) is a City and the headquarters of the Tenkasi district in Indian state of Tamil Nadu.Tenkasi is the largest town in Tenkasi district Being one of the most significant spiritual and cultural places in South Tamilnadu, Tenkasi houses The Kutraleeshwarar temple (Chitra Sabha), one of the five sabhas of Lord Nataraja, an incarnation of Lord Shiva. Tamil poet Thirikooda Rasappa Kavirayar describes this temple in his poem as “Kutrala Kuravanji” which means 'beauty of Kutralam'.
Kuthukalvalasai is a neighborhood located in Tenkasi, Tamil Nadu, India, 1 km away from District headquarters Tenkasi. It is located at the foot of the Western Ghats and Courtallam hills.
Melagaram is a panchayat town in Tenkasi district, in the Indian state of Tamil Nadu. The Famous Composer Thirigudarasapa Kavirayar was born in Melagaram.
Ilanji is a panchayat town in Tenkasi district, in the Indian state of Tamil Nadu. It is located between the towns Tenkasi and Shencottah.
Piranoor is a Panchayat Village of Tenkasi Block and Shenkottai Taluk. in Tenkasi district, Tamil Nadu, India, in the foothills of the Western Ghats.
Aluthakanniar is a river flowing in the Tirunelveli district of the Indian state of Tamil Nadu. It is one of the tributaries of Chittar River, which joins Tamiraparani near Tirunelveli.
Kottakulam is a panchayat town in Tirunelveli district in the Indian state of Tamil Nadu. Kottakulam is aka Sumaitheernthapuram.

Courtallam, natively spelt Kutrālam or Kuttālam, is a panchayat town situated at a mean elevation of 160 m (520 ft) in the foothills of the Western Ghats in Tenkasi district of Tamil Nadu, India. The Coutrallam Falls on the Chittar River is a major tourist attraction.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Kasi Viswanathar temple, Tenkasi?
faq.a: The best time to visit Kasi Viswanathar temple, Tenkasi is Nov – Feb. Summers are 26–36°C and winters are 22–32°C.

faq.q: How do I reach Kasi Viswanathar temple, Tenkasi?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Thiruvananthapuram International Airport (68 km) and the nearest railway station is Thiruvananthapuram Central (65 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Kasi Viswanathar temple, Tenkasi?
faq.a: 3 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Kasi Viswanathar temple, Tenkasi at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Kasi Viswanathar temple, Tenkasi?
faq.a: Stays in Kasi Viswanathar temple, Tenkasi start from ₹900 per night, with options across 4 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in Kasi Viswanathar temple, Tenkasi?
faq.a: The top attractions are Tenkasi, Kuthukalvalasai, Melagaram, Ilanji, Piranoor.
